AboutPage.title: Built for teams who treat APIs as
AboutPage.subtitle: AIAgentsHub powers aiagents-hub.vn — a managed platform for reliable, metered API access. We help developers and enterprises ship faster with clear contracts, observability, and security baked in.

mission.title: Why we exist
mission.lead: Software today runs on APIs. Billing, identity, AI, automation — when the API layer fails, products fail.
mission.body: We focus on predictable performance, transparent usage, and documentation you can actually integrate from day one. Whether you are prototyping or running production traffic, our job is to stay invisible where it matters: uptime, latency, and trust.

values_section.title: What we optimize
values_section.subtitle: Principles that shape how we design endpoints, dashboards, and support.

api_first.title: API-first delivery
api_first.description: Products and pricing are built around clear REST contracts, versioning, and deprecation policies — not afterthoughts.

trust.title: Security & compliance
trust.description: Encryption in transit, least-privilege keys, and audit-friendly controls so teams can meet internal and regulatory expectations.

developer.title: Developer experience
developer.description: Docs with runnable examples, predictable error codes, and tooling that shortens the path from first call to production.

scale.title: Global scale
scale.description: Edge-aware routing and capacity planning so spikes in traffic look like a line chart, not an outage story.

cta.title: Ready to integrate
cta.subtitle: Create an account, pick a package, and start calling production endpoints with metered usage you can track in the dashboard.

CommunityPage.title: A community for teams shipping on
CommunityPage.subtitle: Documentation, support, and clear paths into managed APIs, eKYC, and the dashboard—whether you are prototyping or running production traffic.

steps.3_desc: Call api.aiagents-hub.vn with Bearer tokens, track usage in the dashboard, and reach support when you need humans.

cta.subtitle: Open the dashboard, read the docs, or email us—we route every message to the right owners.

CareersPage.title: Build the backbone of
CareersPage.subtitle: We operate aiagents-hub.vn as a managed API business: reliable gateways, clear metering, and developer-first docs. Help us ship infrastructure other teams depend on every day.

why.title: Why
why.subtitle: Small team, high leverage — typical of an early-stage API product company.

impact.title: Product-shaped work
impact.description: Your code touches authentication, quotas, observability, and customer-facing contracts — not internal-only tools.

ownership.title: Ownership end-to-end
ownership.description: Design, implement, and operate what you build. On-call is light but real; we automate first.

stack.title: Modern stack
stack.description: Cloud-native services, strong typing, metrics everywhere, and docs that stay in sync with the API.

roles_section.title: Open
roles_section.subtitle: Headcount sized for an early startup: a focused core team with room to grow as usage scales.
roles_section.team_total: ~4–5 people across these roles in phase one

api_engineer.title: Backend / API Engineer
api_engineer.description: Design and ship production APIs: versioning, auth, rate limits, metering, and consistent error models. You will collaborate closely on gateway behavior and dashboard-backed usage data.
responsibilities: Own REST/JSON (and future) API surfaces with clear deprecation and compatibility rules.
Implement resilient request paths, idempotency where needed, and observability (traces, metrics, structured logs).
Partner with platform on rollout, feature flags, and safe schema evolution.
Review integrations and help tune performance under real customer load.

devops.title: DevOps / Platform Engineer
devops.description: Keep our API edge reliable: CI/CD, environments, secrets, capacity, and incident tooling. You care about SLOs, not just servers.
responsibilities: Build and maintain pipelines, infrastructure-as-code, and safe deployment practices.
Run monitoring, alerting, and on-call playbooks aligned with API latency and availability targets.
Harden networking, TLS, and access patterns between services and the public gateway.
Improve cost and performance visibility as traffic grows.

dx.title: Developer Experience (DX) Engineer
dx.description: Make integration effortless: documentation, examples, onboarding flows, and crisp communication with partners. You bridge product, support, and engineering.
responsibilities: Own developer docs, quickstarts, and reference material that match real API behavior.
Produce code samples, Postman collections, or SDK snippets that teams can copy-paste with confidence.
Gather feedback from integrators and turn it into actionable improvements.
Support technical conversations with early customers and community channels.

cta.title: Don’t see a perfect fit?
cta.subtitle: We read every message. Send us a few lines about what you want to build — we will reach out when there is a good fit.

quick_actions.recommend_api: Recommend an API for my use case

generate_key.question: How do I generate a new API key?
generate_key.answer: Go to Dashboard > API Keys > Click "New Key". You can name your key and set permissions.

rate_limit.question: What happens if I exceed my rate limit?
rate_limit.answer: Requests exceeding your rate limit will receive a 429 response. Consider upgrading your plan for higher limits.

change_plan.question: How do I change my subscription plan?
change_plan.answer: Navigate to Dashboard > Billing > Change Plan. Changes take effect at your next billing cycle.

free_trial.question: Is there a free trial available?
free_trial.answer: Yes! All our APIs include a free tier with limited calls per month. No credit card required.

need_help.title: Need More Help?
need_help.description: Reach out to our support team

ai_responses.welcome: Hello! I'm your AI support assistant. I can help you with API recommendations, troubleshooting, and answer questions about your usage. How can I assist you today?
ai_responses.recommend: Based on your question, I'd recommend our **AI Vision API** for image work, or our **CLAW APIs** (OpenClaw, NanoClaw, NemoClaw, BizClaw, …) for agents and automation. What specific use case are you working on?
ai_responses.calls: You currently have **15,000 API calls** remaining this month across all your subscriptions. Your quota resets on the 1st of each month.
ai_responses.rate_limit:
Rate limiting protects our infrastructure. Your current limits are:
- **Free tier**: 10 requests/second
- **Pro tier**: 100 requests/second

Consider implementing exponential backoff in your code.
ai_responses.default: I understand you're asking about {query}. Let me connect you with more specific information. Could you provide more details about what you're trying to accomplish?

TermsPage.title: Terms of
TermsPage.subtitle: These Terms govern your use of aiagents-hub.vn and our managed API platform—including AI-assisted and blockchain-related capabilities—in line with common international practice for cloud and API services.
TermsPage.cta_body: Our team can help clarify billing, integration, compliance, or enterprise agreements.

agreement.title: Agreement to these Terms
paragraphs: These Terms of Service ("Terms") form a binding agreement between you ("you", "your", or "Customer") and the operator of aiagents-hub.vn and api.aiagents-hub.vn (collectively, the "Platform", "we", "us", or "our").

By accessing or using the Platform, creating an account, subscribing to an API product, generating API keys, connecting a wallet, or otherwise using our services, you agree to these Terms and to our Privacy Policy (where applicable). If you do not agree, do not use the Platform.
If you use the Platform on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have authority to bind that organization, and "you" includes that entity.

definitions.title: Definitions
paragraphs: "API" means the application programming interfaces, endpoints, SDKs, webhooks, and related developer tools we make available at or through api.aiagents-hub.vn (or successor URLs).
"AI Features" means machine-learning or generative capabilities we provide (for example assistants, classification, enrichment, or automation) whether labeled as AI or not.
"Blockchain Features" means functionality that interacts with public or permissioned distributed ledgers, smart contracts, wallet-based authentication, or on-chain attestations.
"Content" means data, prompts, files, payloads, metadata, or other materials that you or your end users submit to or retrieve from the Platform.

services.title: The services
paragraphs: We provide a managed API business: gateways, metering, documentation, observability hooks, and account tooling intended to help developers and enterprises integrate reliably at scale. Specific capabilities, regions, uptime targets, and support levels depend on your plan and product documentation.
We may modify, deprecate, or replace features where reasonably necessary for security, compliance, or operations, in line with the change and notice practices described below and in product notices.

api_usage.title: API access, keys, and fair use
paragraphs: You must use credentials (API keys, OAuth tokens, or other mechanisms we specify) responsibly and keep them confidential. You are responsible for all activity under your account unless you have notified us of a compromise in line with our security guidance.

We apply rate limits, quotas, concurrency controls, and abuse protections. Responses such as HTTP 429 or equivalent signals indicate that you should back off, optimize usage, or upgrade your plan. Unless expressly stated in a separate written agreement, we do not guarantee unlimited throughput or a particular latency percentile.

You must follow our documentation, versioning, and authentication requirements. We may require migration to newer API versions; continued use after a stated sunset date may be unsupported or disabled.

ai_features.title: AI features and outputs
paragraphs: AI Features may produce probabilistic or incomplete results. Outputs may be inaccurate, outdated, or unsuitable for a given regulated use case. You are solely responsible for evaluating outputs before relying on them in production, safety-critical, financial, medical, legal, or other high-risk contexts.

You must not use AI Features to generate unlawful content, to infringe intellectual property or privacy rights, to deceive end users in material ways, or to bypass reasonable safeguards we implement. Where we describe restrictions for specific models or tools, those restrictions form part of these Terms.

Unless we expressly agree otherwise in writing, we do not use your Content to train foundation models in a way that identifies you or your end users; however, we may process Content to provide the service, secure the Platform, comply with law, and improve reliability (for example logging, debugging, and aggregated quality metrics).

blockchain.title: Blockchain, wallets, and on-chain activity
paragraphs: Blockchain Features depend on third-party networks that we do not control. Transaction finality, fees (including gas), reorganizations, smart-contract behavior, and token volatility are outside our responsibility. You acknowledge the inherent risks of interacting with blockchain systems.

Wallet connections and signatures are your responsibility. You must safeguard seed phrases, private keys, and devices. We are not a custodian of your digital assets unless a separate written agreement explicitly states otherwise.

When Blockchain Features involve reading or writing on-chain data, you represent that your use complies with applicable sanctions, securities, consumer-protection, and anti-money-laundering rules that apply to you. We may block, filter, or refuse interactions where required by law or to protect the Platform.

acceptable_use.title: Acceptable use
paragraphs: You agree not to misuse the Platform. Without limitation, you must not probe, scan, or attack our systems; circumvent technical limits; distribute malware; send spam; harvest data in violation of applicable law; or use the APIs in a way that endangers minors, promotes violence, or violates export-control or sanctions rules.

You must not resell or sublicense API access in a manner that misrepresents origin, evades metering, or violates your plan. If you offer a product to your own customers powered by our APIs, you remain responsible for your product and for passing appropriate terms and disclosures to your users.
We may investigate suspected violations and suspend or terminate access where reasonably necessary to protect users, the Platform, or comply with legal obligations.

accounts.title: Accounts and security
paragraphs: You must provide accurate registration information and keep it current. Multi-factor authentication and least-privilege API keys are strongly encouraged.
You must notify us promptly of unauthorized access or suspected key leakage. We may take protective actions such as rotating credentials, throttling traffic, or requiring additional verification.

fees.title: Fees, taxes, and billing
paragraphs: Paid plans, usage-based charges, and taxes (where applicable) are described at checkout, in your dashboard, or in an order form. Unless stated otherwise, fees are non-refundable except where mandatory law requires otherwise.
Failure to pay may result in suspension or termination of access. You are responsible for providing valid payment details and for any bank or blockchain network fees charged by third parties.

ip.title: Intellectual property
paragraphs: We retain all rights in the Platform, documentation, trademarks, and our proprietary models and software, except for rights expressly granted to you.

We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license during your subscription to call the APIs and use our documentation solely as needed to exercise your rights under these Terms. You may not reverse engineer our services except where mandatory law permits interoperability.

You grant us a worldwide license to host, process, transmit, and display your Content as reasonably necessary to provide and secure the services. You represent that you have the rights needed to grant this license.

data.title: Data protection and privacy
paragraphs: Our processing of personal data is described in our Privacy Policy and, where relevant, data processing terms offered to enterprise customers. You will not submit special categories of personal data to the APIs unless permitted by law and contract.
We may generate service logs and metrics (including request metadata and error traces) to operate, secure, and improve the Platform. Retention follows our policies and legal requirements.

warranties.title: Disclaimers
paragraphs: EXCEPT WHERE PROHIBITED BY LAW, THE PLATFORM AND APIs ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

WE DO NOT WARRANT THAT THE SERVICES WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED, ERROR-FREE, OR FREE OF HARMFUL COMPONENTS. AI AND BLOCKCHAIN SYSTEMS INVOLVE INHERENT UNCERTAINTY; YOU ASSUME ALL RISKS ASSOCIATED WITH YOUR USE.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
paragraphs: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, GOODWILL, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY.

OUR AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THESE TERMS OR THE SERVICES WILL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF (A) THE AMOUNTS YOU PAID TO US FOR THE SERVICES IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS BEFORE THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO LIABILITY, OR (B) ONE HUNDRED U.S. DOLLARS (USD $100) IF YOU HAVE NOT HAD A PAYMENT OBLIGATION. MULTIPLE CLAIMS WILL NOT EXPAND THIS CAP. SOME JURISDICTIONS DO NOT ALLOW CERTAIN LIMITATIONS; IN THOSE CASES OUR LIABILITY IS LIMITED TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED.

indemnity.title: Indemnity
paragraphs: You will defend and indemnify us and our affiliates, officers, and employees against third-party claims, damages, and costs (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from your Content, your products, your misuse of the APIs, or your violation of these Terms or applicable law, except to the extent caused by our willful misconduct.

changes.title: Changes to these Terms
paragraphs: We may update these Terms to reflect new features, legal requirements, or risk management needs. We will post the revised Terms on this page and update the "Last updated" date. Where changes are material, we will provide additional notice when reasonable (for example email or dashboard banner).
Continued use after the effective date of updated Terms constitutes acceptance. If you do not agree, you must stop using the Platform and cancel paid subscriptions according to our billing rules.

law.title: Governing law and disputes
paragraphs: Unless mandatory consumer-protection rules in your jurisdiction require otherwise, these Terms are governed by the laws applicable to the operator of aiagents-hub.vn, without regard to conflict-of-law principles that would require applying another jurisdiction's laws.

You agree to first contact us to seek an informal resolution of any dispute. If a dispute cannot be resolved informally, exclusive jurisdiction and venue for litigation will lie with the courts competent for the operator's registered seat, unless applicable law requires a different forum for consumers.
The United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods does not apply.

contact.title: Contact
paragraphs: For questions about these Terms, billing, or compliance, contact us at support@aiagents-hub.vn. Corporate name and registered address may be provided upon request for contractual or regulatory purposes.

PrivacyPage.title: Privacy
PrivacyPage.subtitle: How we collect, use, share, and protect personal information when you use aiagents-hub.vn, api.aiagents-hub.vn, and related services. This notice is designed to align with common international expectations, including transparency principles reflected in the GDPR and similar frameworks.
PrivacyPage.cta_title: Questions about privacy or your data?
PrivacyPage.cta_body: Reach out for data-protection questions, enterprise agreements, or to exercise your privacy rights.

intro.title: Introduction and scope
paragraphs: This Privacy Policy ("Policy") explains how the operator of aiagents-hub.vn and api.aiagents-hub.vn (collectively, the "Platform", "we", "us", or "our") handles personal information.

It applies when you visit our websites, create or use an account, purchase or use API products, interact with dashboards or documentation, authenticate with a wallet or passkey where offered, or otherwise engage with our services. It should be read together with our Terms of Service and any product-specific notices we provide.

We may update this Policy from time to time. We will post the revised version on this page and change the "Last updated" date. Where changes are material, we will provide additional notice when reasonable (for example by email or an in-product banner).

controller.title: Data controller and contact
paragraphs: The data controller responsible for personal information described in this Policy is the legal entity operating the Platform (referred to here as "AIAgentsHub" / "Unitoken" for convenience). Corporate name, registered address, and registration identifiers may be provided on request for contractual, invoicing, or regulatory purposes.

For general privacy inquiries and to exercise your rights where applicable, contact us at support@aiagents-hub.vn. For security incidents involving credentials or suspected unauthorized access, contact us promptly using the same channel and include relevant details so we can respond effectively.

collection.title: Categories of information we collect
paragraphs: Account and identity data: such as name, email address, organization, username or user ID, authentication factors you enable (for example TOTP, passkeys, or linked social login identifiers), and preferences you set in your profile.

Usage and technical data: such as IP address, approximate location derived from IP, device and browser type, timestamps, pages or endpoints accessed, API request metadata (for example route, status codes, latency, error traces), subscription and metering records, support tickets, and in-product analytics needed to operate and improve the service.

Payment and billing data: such as billing contact details, transaction references, tax identifiers where required, and payment status. Payment card processing may be handled by certified payment partners; we typically receive limited billing and confirmation data rather than full card numbers.
Communications: content you send us (for example emails, contact forms, or chat transcripts) and records needed to respond and demonstrate compliance.

Blockchain- and wallet-related data: where you connect a wallet or use on-chain features, we may process public wallet addresses, chain identifiers, and transaction or attestation references as needed to provide those features. Public blockchain data is inherently visible on networks we do not control.
Cookies and similar technologies: described in the section on cookies below.

purposes.title: Purposes and legal bases
paragraphs: We process personal information for purposes such as: providing, securing, and improving the Platform; authenticating users; metering and billing; customer support; fraud prevention, abuse detection, and security monitoring; complying with legal obligations; and communicating service-related notices.

Depending on the context and applicable law, we rely on one or more of the following legal bases: performance of a contract with you; legitimate interests that are not overridden by your rights (for example securing our systems and understanding aggregate usage), where permitted; compliance with legal obligations; and consent where required (for example certain non-essential cookies or marketing, where we ask for it separately).
Where consent is the basis, you may withdraw it at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing before withdrawal. Marketing communications, if any, will include a way to opt out.

cookies.title: Cookies and similar technologies
paragraphs: We use cookies, local storage, and similar technologies for session management, security (for example CSRF protection), preferences (such as language or theme), and analytics that help us understand how the Platform is used in aggregate.

You can control cookies through your browser settings. Blocking strictly necessary cookies may affect login or security features. Where required, we will obtain consent before using non-essential cookies; until then, only necessary cookies may run.
Our Cookie Policy at /cookies describes categories, purposes, and retention in more detail.

sharing.title: Disclosure and subprocessors
paragraphs: We do not sell your personal information in the conventional sense of exchanging data for money. We may share information with service providers who process data on our instructions (for example cloud hosting, observability, email delivery, payment processing, customer support tooling, and security services).

We may disclose information if required by law, regulation, legal process, or governmental request; to enforce our terms or protect rights, safety, and security; or in connection with a merger, acquisition, or asset transfer, subject to appropriate safeguards and notice where required.
An up-to-date list of key subprocessors or categories may be provided in your dashboard, order form, or enterprise data processing agreement where applicable.

transfers.title: International transfers
paragraphs: We may process and store information in countries other than where you live, including where our infrastructure and partners operate. When we transfer personal information from the European Economic Area, the United Kingdom, or Switzerland to countries not subject to an adequacy decision, we implement appropriate safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission (and UK/Swiss equivalents where relevant) or other lawful transfer mechanisms.
You may request more information about these safeguards by contacting us at support@aiagents-hub.vn.

retention.title: Retention
paragraphs: We retain personal information only as long as necessary for the purposes described in this Policy, unless a longer period is required or permitted by law (for example tax, accounting, or dispute resolution).

Factors include the nature of the data, ongoing business needs (such as active subscriptions), security and troubleshooting logs with limited retention, and anonymization or aggregation where feasible for analytics.

security.title: Security
paragraphs: We implement technical and organizational measures designed to protect personal information against unauthorized access, loss, or alteration. These may include encryption in transit, access controls, least-privilege practices for staff and systems, monitoring, and vendor assessments.
No method of transmission or storage is completely secure. You are responsible for safeguarding your credentials, API keys, and wallet seed phrases. Please report suspected incidents to us promptly.

rights.title: Your privacy rights
paragraphs: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or export personal information; restrict or object to certain processing; withdraw consent where processing is consent-based; and lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.

Residents of the European Economic Area, the United Kingdom, and similar jurisdictions can exercise these rights subject to applicable exceptions. We will respond within the timeframes required by law, typically within one month for GDPR requests (extensions may apply for complex requests).

If you are a California resident, you may have rights under the CCPA/CPRA, including to know categories of personal information collected, purposes, sources, disclosures, and to request correction or deletion subject to exceptions. We do not "sell" or "share" personal information as those terms are defined in the CPRA for targeted advertising in a way that requires opt-out of sale/sharing for the core API service described here; if our practices change, we will update this Policy and any required links.

To submit a request, email support@aiagents-hub.vn from your account email or provide information we can use to verify your identity. Authorized agents may submit requests where permitted by law with appropriate documentation.

children.title: Children
paragraphs: The Platform is not directed at children under the age where parental consent is required for online services in their jurisdiction (often 13 or 16). We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe we have collected such information, contact us and we will take appropriate steps to delete it.

automated.title: Automated decision-making
paragraphs: We do not make decisions based solely on automated processing, including profiling, that produce legal or similarly significant effects concerning you, unless we are required to do so by law or we notify you otherwise and offer suitable safeguards.

Some security, fraud-prevention, or metering systems may use automated signals (for example rate limits or anomaly scores); these are generally operational safeguards rather than solely automated decisions about your legal rights.

changes.title: Changes to this Policy
paragraphs: We may revise this Policy to reflect changes in our practices, technologies, or legal requirements. The updated version will be posted on this page with a new "Last updated" date. We encourage you to review it periodically.

contact.title: Contact
paragraphs: For privacy questions, data protection requests, or concerns about this Policy, contact support@aiagents-hub.vn. You may also contact your local data protection authority if you believe applicable law has been violated.

CookiesPage.title: Cookie
CookiesPage.subtitle: How aiagents-hub.vn and api.aiagents-hub.vn use cookies and similar technologies for authentication, security, wallet features, and preferences. This page complements our Privacy Policy and reflects how our services are built today.
CookiesPage.inventory_footnote: Cookie names, lifetimes, and security flags may be adjusted as we ship updates; we will update this page when changes are material. HttpOnly cookies are not readable by page scripts in the browser.
CookiesPage.cta_body: Contact us for clarification on authentication flows, enterprise requirements, or data protection requests.

intro.title: Introduction
paragraphs: This Cookie Policy explains how the operator of aiagents-hub.vn and api.aiagents-hub.vn (the "Platform", "we", "us") uses HTTP cookies and similar storage on your device when you use our websites, dashboards, and APIs.

It should be read together with our Privacy Policy, which describes personal data processing more broadly. Where this page refers to "cookies", we include comparable technologies such as local storage when they serve a similar role, unless noted otherwise.

We minimize data collection and rely on strictly necessary cookies for core security and session management. Non-essential cookies or analytics run only where compatible with our product configuration and applicable law.

what_are_cookies.title: What cookies are
paragraphs: Cookies are small text files placed on your device by a web server. First-party cookies are set by domains we control (for example aiagents-hub.vn or api.aiagents-hub.vn). Third-party cookies may be set by embedded services or providers when you use certain features.

Cookies can be session cookies (removed when you close the browser) or persistent cookies (stored until they expire or you delete them). Attributes such as HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite help limit how cookies are accessed and sent across sites.

purposes.title: Why we use cookies
paragraphs: We use cookies to: keep you signed in securely; protect accounts against abuse; complete OAuth, OTP, and wallet handshakes; remember UI preferences where we store them in cookies; and operate diagnostics that keep the Platform reliable.

We classify cookies as strictly necessary (required for security and basic operation), functional (improving experience, such as wallet state), and, where enabled, analytics (aggregate insight). We do not use cookies to sell personal information in the conventional sense described in our Privacy Policy.

inventory.title: Cookie inventory (first-party)
paragraphs: The table below lists first-party cookies our authentication and application layers commonly set for domains under .aiagents-hub.vn. They are sent with Secure and SameSite=Lax unless legacy compatibility requires otherwise, and use the shared registrable domain so sign-in works across subdomains.

items.name: sessionId
items.purpose: Maintains your authenticated session with the API after login; HttpOnly, not accessible to scripts.
items.duration: Per session configuration (hours to days, per system settings)

items.name: preAuthSessionId
items.purpose: Short-lived session for OAuth redirects, OTP verification, and wallet connect flows before full authentication.

items.name: didChallengeId
items.purpose: Stores a decentralized identity (DID) linking challenge identifier during wallet-based identity flows.

items.name: token, refreshToken
items.purpose: Legacy access and refresh tokens where still in use; cleared when migrating fully to session-based cookies.

items.name: Wagmi storage (name may vary)
items.purpose: Persists Web3 wallet connection state for dashboard features using Wagmi cookie storage (WalletConnect and related flows).
items.duration: Session or as configured by Wagmi in the browser

web3_ui.title: Wallet, dashboard, and preferences
paragraphs: When you connect a wallet, WalletConnect and chain providers may set or read additional cookies or device storage to complete pairing, display chain state, or meet their security requirements. Those providers process data under their own policies.

Theme, language, or layout preferences may be stored in cookies or local storage depending on the surface (marketing site vs dashboard). Blocking functional storage may reset non-essential preferences on each visit.

third_party.title: Third-party and infrastructure
paragraphs: Our infrastructure (for example edge networks and observability) may emit technical cookies or identifiers needed for load balancing, bot management, or resilience. Payment processors may set cookies when you complete checkout on their pages.

We do not intentionally load third-party advertising trackers on core API or dashboard surfaces for behavioral profiling. If we introduce optional analytics or marketing cookies that require consent in your region, we will describe them here and provide controls where required.

control.title: Your choices and browser controls
paragraphs: You can delete or block cookies through your browser settings. Blocking strictly necessary cookies will prevent sign-in or may break security-sensitive flows.

Industry opt-out tools for interest-based ads are generally not central to our API platform; if you enable "Do Not Track" or Global Privacy Control, we treat those signals where our stack supports them, but some features may still require necessary cookies to function.

changes.title: Changes to this Cookie Policy
paragraphs: We may update this page to reflect new features, partner integrations, or legal requirements. The "Last updated" date will change when we publish edits. Material changes may also be communicated through the dashboard or email when appropriate.

contact.title: Contact
paragraphs: Questions about this Cookie Policy or similar technologies: support@aiagents-hub.vn.

ekyc-how-we-deliver-identity-verificatio.title: How we deliver eKYC as a managed service
ekyc-how-we-deliver-identity-verificatio.excerpt: A concise look at verification stages, evidence handling, and how we keep the experience predictable for integrators.
body: Electronic Know Your Customer (eKYC) is no longer a nice-to-have: it is the front door for regulated onboarding, payouts, and high-trust workflows. At aiagents-hub.vn we treat eKYC as a product surface with explicit API contracts, not a one-off integration project.

Our service separates capture, verification, and decisioning into well-defined steps. That means your application can collect user consent and documents once, while our pipeline applies consistent checks, machine-assisted review where appropriate, and auditable outcomes you can reconcile in your own systems.

Operationally, we design for resilience: idempotent requests where it matters, structured errors that map to remediation paths, and dashboards that reflect status without forcing support tickets. The goal is simple — your team ships faster without trading off clarity for customers.

If you are evaluating providers, ask how outcomes are represented, how retention is bounded, and how keys are scoped. We publish those expectations alongside our endpoints so compliance and engineering can review the same facts.

ekyc-api-contracts-for-international-rol.title: eKYC API contracts built for international rollouts
ekyc-api-contracts-for-international-rol.excerpt: Versioning, scopes, and error models that match how serious teams ship identity checks across regions.
body: International rollouts fail when APIs are ambiguous. We version resources deliberately, document breaking changes in advance, and align authentication with least-privilege scopes so a key used for sandbox cannot accidentally reach production data.
Payloads favor explicit fields over overloaded enums. Where regional rules differ, we expose capability flags and supported document types so your client can branch without guessing server behavior.

Errors are machine-readable first: stable codes, human text second. That pattern is standard among cloud platforms for a reason — it keeps mobile, web, and server clients aligned when something needs a retry or a user-facing correction.

For partners planning phased launches, we recommend starting with a narrow verification profile, measuring conversion and latency, then expanding document coverage. Our contracts are designed to grow with you without rewriting your core integration.

ekyc-data-minimization-retention-and-aud.title: Data minimization, retention, and audit-ready eKYC
ekyc-data-minimization-retention-and-aud.excerpt: Why we collect only what verification requires, how retention windows are communicated, and what auditors typically review.
body: Strong eKYC is as much about what you do not retain as what you collect. We apply data minimization by default: sensitive elements are processed for verification, then retained only where policy or regulation requires a limited audit trail.

Retention is not an afterthought. Windows are communicated in product terms and reflected operationally — your security reviews should see the same story our engineers implement. When customers request deletion workflows, we align responses with applicable timelines and verification steps.

Auditability means traceability without unnecessary exposure. Access to verification artifacts is logged, scoped to roles, and compatible with periodic reviews. That is the baseline teams expect when identity touches regulated flows.

If your program spans multiple jurisdictions, pair our technical controls with your legal assessment. We provide the operational detail; your counsel maps it to local rules — a division of labor that scales internationally.

claw-roadmap-openclaw-nanoclaw-nemoclaw-.title: CLAW on aiagents-hub.vn: roadmap and upcoming capabilities
claw-roadmap-openclaw-nanoclaw-nemoclaw-.excerpt: Where we are taking CLAW next — multi-variant support, routing, and event-driven integrations for automation-heavy stacks.
body: CLAW is our upcoming connectivity layer for agent-style and automation workloads across OpenClaw, NanoClaw, NemoClaw, BizClaw, and compatible implementations. The objective is familiar to platform teams: integrate once, route intelligently, and observe behavior consistently.

On the roadmap you will see first-class configuration per CLAW variant, clearer separation between synchronous calls and long-running tasks, and webhooks that reflect state transitions you can trust for downstream orchestration.

We are also tightening developer ergonomics: predictable authentication aligned with the rest of api.aiagents-hub.vn, sandbox parity that mirrors production limits, and documentation that stays synchronized with the API surface.
Nothing here replaces your security review — it complements it. As we approach general availability, we will publish migration notes, deprecation policies, and support channels for design partners.

claw-launch-what-partners-can-expect.title: What partners can expect as CLAW services go live
claw-launch-what-partners-can-expect.excerpt: Timelines at a high level, scope boundaries, and how onboarding will work for early adopters.
body: Partners should expect a staged rollout: private previews with explicit SLAs, followed by broader access as we validate throughput, failure modes, and operational playbooks. We will communicate cutover dates with enough lead time for your runbooks.

Scope will be clear. Each preview window will list supported CLAW variants, regions, and rate limits. Features marked experimental will be labeled as such — we will not silently promote behavior that your integration depended on as provisional.
Onboarding will mirror how we onboard eKYC integrators today: technical contacts, a shared channel for incidents, and documentation that includes worked examples rather than abstract promises.

If you plan to depend on CLAW for customer-facing automation, start by mapping your failure domains now — retries, idempotency keys, and user-visible messaging. The platform will meet you with observability; your product must still explain delays kindly.

claw-integration-patterns-early-access.title: Integration patterns for CLAW: early access considerations
claw-integration-patterns-early-access.excerpt: Queue-backed workers, event subscriptions, and safe testing strategies before you wire production traffic.
body: Early CLAW integrations benefit from the same patterns that work for mature APIs: isolate outbound calls behind your own queue, cap concurrency, and subscribe to events instead of polling when possible — it reduces load and simplifies recovery.
In previews, we may expose additional diagnostic headers or trace identifiers. Use them while they exist, but avoid hard-coding reliance until those fields graduate to stable documentation.

Testing should span sandbox scenarios that include partial failures. Automation that never sees a timeout is automation that will surprise you later. We will publish recommended test matrices alongside reference implementations.

Interested teams can reach out via support@aiagents-hub.vn with a short summary of use case, expected volume, and regions. We prioritize partners who can share structured feedback during the preview window.
